untranslatable_reason: Signal requires dynamic position sizing ratios (low_iv_buy_ratio, low_iv_sell_ratio, high_iv_buy_ratio, high_iv_sell_ratio) which cannot be expressed as simple entry/exit conditions. The rule is a portfolio construction strategy adjusting leverage and directional exposure based on IV regime, not a discrete entry/exit trigger. IV levels must be inferred via hvol_20 as a proxy, but the core mechanic—adjusting option purchase vs sale ratios—requires position management logic outside backtest engine scope.
translation_notes: Used hvol_20 (20-period historical volatility) as proxy for implied volatility regime detection. LOW_IV regime approximated as hvol_20 < 0.15; HIGH_IV regime approximated as hvol_20 > 0.25. Neutral zone assumed around hvol_20 = 0.20. Original signal from Natenberg context discusses delta-hedging and position sizing ratios which are position-level mechanics, not entry/exit signals. This translation captures IV regime detection but loses the ratio adjustment component.
